findings: The diff removes package-lock.json entirely, which changes dependency resolution behavior and can affect build/deploy reproducibility; there are no tests or verification steps covering this packaging change.

Test Suggestion 1: Run a clean install/build in a fresh environment (no node_modules, no lockfile) and verify the app still compiles and starts successfully, because deleting the lockfile can surface hidden dependency drift.

Test Suggestion 2: Validate the Vercel preview deployment path end-to-end with the exact CI install command used by Vercel, ensuring the build succeeds and produces the expected output, since the PR is specifically about preview deploy failures.

Test Suggestion 3: Compare resolved dependency versions before and after the lockfile removal for critical packages like Next.js, React, and Tailwind, and run a smoke test of a representative page to catch runtime regressions caused by transitive version changes.

No security issues detected in the provided diff. The only change shown is deletion of `package-lock.json`; there are no modifications to authentication/authorization logic, input handling, or secret handling.
Primary driver: no executable code changes; only deletion of package-lock.json

P1: Removing package-lock.json changes dependency resolution and can make Vercel preview builds non-reproducible; verify the exact CI install/build path in a clean environment and confirm the app still compiles and starts [TestQuality] - Re-run the preview deploy flow end-to-end with Vercel's install command before merging.

P2: Compare resolved versions for critical packages after lockfile removal and smoke test a representative page to catch transitive dependency regressions [TestQuality] - Pin or restore the lockfile if version drift causes instability.
